name: Trint
description: Transcription and collaborative editing platform for media teams

developer.comment: London-based company founded in 2014 by Emmy-winning broadcast journalist Jeff Kofman with Mark Boas and James Edwards.

first-release.comment: Founded 2014; only year-level precision is reliably documented for the first public release, so month is approximate. Stored as year.

status.comment: Actively developed; current product, pricing and help pages live as of June 2026.

license.comment: Closed-source commercial SaaS with no permanent free tier (trial only), so proprietary rather than freemium.

primary-type.comment: Core job is automated transcription with collaborative editorial editing for newsrooms/media teams; not a speak-to-type dictation tool or a meeting-bot assistant.

real-time-dictation.comment: Trint transcribes recordings/uploads and can transcribe live streams, but it is not a speak-to-type dictation input method that types into other apps.

file-transcription.comment: Core feature: upload audio/video files (MP3, WAV, M4A, MP4, MOV) or import from YouTube/Vimeo and transcribe them.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting bot that joins calls live; the Zoom integration auto-imports finished cloud recordings (English only), which is file transcription rather than live capture.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Realtime Transcription produces a running transcript of a live stream with transcripts appearing about three seconds after speech.

audio-sources.comment: Records from mic (mobile/live), accepts file uploads, and imports from URLs (YouTube/Vimeo); no bot that joins meetings and no general system-audio capture documented.

underlying-engine.comment: Trint markets its own proprietary AI/ASR but does not clearly disclose a specific named recognition engine, so left null per RESEARCH.md (do not infer).

language-count.comment: Approx; Trint states transcription in 'more than 50 languages'. Some third-party reviews cite ~30, but the vendor's current figure is 50+; translation covers 70+ languages.

english-accuracy.comment: Trint markets up to 99% accuracy; independent review testing found closer to ~90% on clear English (no formal published WER). Qualitative 4/5.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Live app 'can detect more than 50 languages and switch between different languages while transcribing live'.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Vocab Builder / custom dictionary lets users add a list of up to 100 words/names to improve recognition on later transcripts.

speaker-diarization.comment: Speaker Detection at upload separates speakers automatically; Auto Speaker Recognition enrolls voiceprints to auto-label named speakers on future transcripts.

timestamps.comment: Word/segment timestamps in the editor underpin word-by-word playback and SRT/VTT subtitle export.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: AI Assistant summarizes, analyzes and extracts key moments/soundbites/insights from transcripts via text prompts.

built-in-editor.comment: In-app collaborative transcript editor with word-by-word playback for review and correction; text-based editing, not media-by-text editing like Descript.

translation.comment: Translate transcripts into more than 70 languages, including instant translation of live transcripts.

export-formats.comment: Word (DOCX), CSV, subtitles (SRT, VTT, STL), and editing formats (EDL, Premiere XML); JSON transcript export available via the developer API. No native PDF; plain-text export is via Avid DS .txt subtitle format rather than a generic TXT option.

processing-location.comment: Audio is processed in Trint's cloud on AWS; users can choose EU or US storage region. No on-device option.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud-based; requires a network connection to transcribe.

data-used-for-training.comment: Trint states 'we never listen to your recordings to train our algorithms', training instead on external data sets.

data-deletion-control.comment: Data retained for the subscription lifetime by default; secure deletion can be arranged at contract end or once per month on request. GDPR compliant.

platforms.comment: Web app plus iOS and Android mobile apps; no native desktop (macOS/Windows/Linux) app, though an Adobe Premiere plugin exists.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; no OS-wide speak-to-type into other applications.

integrations.comment: Zoom auto-import (English recordings only) maps to a defined tag; also integrates with Adobe Premiere, AP ENPS and Mimir, which have no tag in the defined set.

api-automation.comment: Documented developer platform with REST and Realtime APIs (separate EU/US endpoints) for uploading, transcribing and exporting programmatically.

pricing-model.comment: Per-seat subscription tiers (Starter/Advanced/Enterprise), annual billing required on most paid tiers; no genuine free plan.

free-tier.comment: No permanent free plan in 2026 — only a 7-day free trial (3 files, first 5 minutes of each transcribed).

free-allowance.comment: No free tier; the only free access is a time-limited 7-day trial, so no ongoing free allowance.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan (Starter) is ~$80/seat/mo with annual billing, capped at 7 files/seat/mo. Advanced is ~$100/seat/mo for unlimited files. Checked June 2026; vendor pricing page was not publicly reachable, so figures are from recent secondary reviews.
